summary: This is a brief announcement post where Cathy O'Neil shares her NPR Morning Edition appearance discussing alternatives to Facebook. The post links to the NPR segment exploring what a better social network might look like in light of Facebook's flaws. No substantive argument is made in the post itself, as the content exists in the linked audio segment.
key_points: O'Neil appeared on NPR's Morning Edition to discuss social media alternatives
The discussion centered on whether relying on a single dominant social network is problematic
The segment explored what a better social network might look like
money_quotes: Would We Be Better Off If We Didn't Rely On 1 Social Network?
key_insight: The dominance of a single social network like Facebook raises questions about whether alternative models might better serve society.
